source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

source: D68685 HAL 4.0 Cybersecurity Statement Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. source: D54550 HAL 4.0 Validation Plan Rev 05.docx.pdf
title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User.  Activities performed per appropriate STI’s.  Validate and update STI documentation  Findings are recorded in a Test Report and issues submitted. HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 Validation Plan

source: D78333 Ethos 4.0 Description of interoperability Study Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 1. Basic Information > 2.2 Interoperation Maintenance
text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service

source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User.  Activities performed per appropriate STI’s.  Validate and update STI documentation  Findings are recorded in a Test Report and issues submitted. HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 Validation Plan

source: D40348 HAL 4.0 TSCL for SN 4 Release 4.00.0122 Rev14.pdf
title: 4.0 verification and validation testing.
text_content: Verification Testing Halcyon SN4 was built per Pre-production Phase I (Silver) DMR, and is maintained in the production equivalent configuration using Discrepancy Report (DR) Handling and Engineering Change (EC) and QNotes processes. Issues that are found during testing are logged as DRs, and the impact to verification and validation testing is assessed. This assessment defines whether the part needs to be replaced or reworked in the machines after implementation of the design change. This decision is documented in the DR. The hardware changes are implemented, and the final material dispositioning (per DR) is done via EC process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The Device Parts List of the verification devices is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. Validation Testing Halcyon SN4 was upgraded to the final production equivalent configuration (defined by Gold DMR).

text_content: The software used in this product strictly follows Varian's established design and development process. Complete a complete set of document updates for non-clinical software features (display of new Halcyon Evolve models in software AboutBox), specifically covering software requirement specification, traceability analysis, validation and verification, and cybersecurity of self-developed software. There are no changes in risk management, architecture diagram, software design specification and interoperability. The corresponding unchanged statement document has been submitted simultaneously. In conclusion, there is no change in the safety and effectiveness of this software. text_content: If it is a major enhancement, change component XX and adjust the version number. If this is a minor enhancement change component YY and adjust the version number. If this is a corrective update, change component ZZ and adjust the version number. Major enhancement: Examples of major release changes would include changes to: Indications for Use and/or Contraindications; changes in computing platform (e.g. Windows to IOS); major new clinical functionalities or major performance enhancement of medical data handling; changes in security changes in software safety class (A, B, C) or in mandatory standards or in safety hazards or innovative workflow altering user habits Minor enhancement: Examples of minor release changes would include additional enhancements that do not affect security, safety or efficacy. Corrective update: Examples of maintenance release changes would include immediate fixes (hot fixes). 2.7.2 Revision History Build#DateSignificant Differences from Previous Version
